purpose: Decorate an input spline (text + boundary spline → Connect Splines stack) with procedural Victorian-style scrollwork curls/ornaments wherever C4D Field falloff overlaps the spline. Field motion (keyframed Linear Fields + Random Field) gates WHERE growth happens.
visual_result: Beautiful filigree ornaments — curls, leaves, flourishes — appear along the input spline path WHERE fields overlap. As fields move (animated keyframes), the ornament distribution shifts. Confirmed by manually positioning fields LEFT vs RIGHT vs CENTERED — growth follows.
input_geometry: Connect Splines (Text Spline + Spline) feeding into Spline Mask combined with a Tracer/Fracture/Connect Instance pipeline. ALL classic-OM generators feeding the Neutron host as children.

hosts.role: procedural ornament generator on input spline

annotation_storage_pattern: No scaffold@ section labels in this graph — naming is on memory/loop/assembler/etc. directly.
am_exposure_pattern_note: Same root-direct (no floatingio) pattern as scenes 04 and 05. 8 generic 'Input' ports — UX anti-pattern.

key_connections.purpose: Read OM children (Connect Splines + Spline Mask) — variable-count input.
Per-segment iteration — each segment processed independently in the LCV body.
Read C4D Field-painted vertex weights — drives per-segment growth amplitude.
Accumulate per-segment curl points into global Points/Segments arrays.
Per-vertex color along the resulting spline (orange→yellow visible on input).

output_routing.host_visibility_path: Object Group's output is a Null containing dozens of LineObjects — the input plus all curl decorations.
output_routing.notes: Output cache is hierarchical: a Null parent with ~30+ child SplineObjects. Same pattern as scene 05's multi-segment output but with much richer per-segment shape variation.

interesting_types: Multi-LineObject output (Null + many SplineObject children) for multi-curl rendering
Per-vertex color on output spline (gradient@ + color@) — drives material color procedurally
splitspline@ for per-segment iteration (parallel to explode_islands@ for meshes)
Field-painted vertex selection as growth gating mechanism
Classic-OM generators (Connect/Tracer/Fracture/Spline Mask) as input children — graph composes with rest of C4D

core_idea_nodes.name: Loop
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Per-evaluation iteration over spline segments (NOT per-frame state). Produces curl geometry in body.

core_idea_nodes.name: Divide Spline
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Splits an input spline into per-segment chunks for independent processing. Like explode_islands@ for meshes — the canonical 'process each piece independently' primitive for splines.

core_idea_nodes.name: Lines Topology Get
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Reads spline topology (segment counts, per-segment point ranges). Lets the graph dispatch correctly when the input has unknown segment structure.

core_idea_nodes.name: Points Info
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: C4D Field → graph bridge. Reads field-painted vertex weights as scalar data. Same pattern as scene 03.

core_idea_nodes.name: Children Op
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: OM-children enumeration. Same primitive as scene 05. Reads classic-OM generators (Connect Splines, Spline Mask) as input.

core_idea_nodes.name: Assemble Spline
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Same multi-segment spline output primitive as scene 05. Confirms R12 is the universal Nodes-Spline output recipe.

scaffolding_nodes: multransform_5 / combine / mat / sqrpart / sqrtrans / vectrans (root template internals)
what_is_clever: OM-driven animation, stateless graph: keyframed Fields + classic Tracer/Fracture animations live in OM; the graph stays pure. No memory@ complexity, no sequential-stepping requirement.
splitspline@ for per-spline-segment processing — like explode_islands@ for meshes.
lineget@ topology introspection lets the graph generalize over unknown spline structures.
Classic OM generators (Connect/Tracer/Fracture/Spline Mask) compose naturally as host input children. Scene Nodes is not an island — it lives alongside the rest of C4D.
Same assembler@ Points+Segments output pattern as scene 05 — universal Nodes-Spline output.
Per-vertex color emission (gradient@ + color@) lets the spline carry material data without external textures.

gotchas.issue: Flat 2D output (splines lying on a plane, vertex maps, etc.) is INVISIBLE from a side-view camera at y=0 — the camera sees the geometry edge-on. Default StudyCam at z=-1500, y=0 made my early frame captures appear empty.
gotchas.evidence: Initial captures with side camera showed only field gizmos + input spline. Moving camera to top-down (y=+1500 looking down) revealed the full ornament. Spenser explicitly corrected this — the Linear Fields are keyframed correctly from f0 onward; my side camera was just edge-on to the flat output. Methodology gotcha #58.
gotchas.workaround: For procedural-spline scenes or anything outputting flat 2D content, default to TOP-DOWN viewport framing. Use cam.SetAbsPos(center + Vector(0, max_radius * 2, 0)) + SetRelRot(0, -90deg, 0) before screenshots.

gotchas.issue: Manually moving fields IS a valid behavioral test for field-driven scenes (and was used to demonstrate field→growth correspondence — left/right shifts produce left/right ornament distribution).
gotchas.evidence: fields_centered_topdown.png shows full coverage; fields_left.png shows growth on left half; fields_right.png shows growth on right half. Independent of the keyframed animation, this proves the architecture.
gotchas.workaround: Use field repositioning as a sanity check for field-driven scenes — but don't conclude the scene is broken just because curls aren't visible from a side camera.

gotchas.issue: Output is a Null with many SplineObject children, not a single LineObject. cache.GetPointCount() returns None on the parent.
gotchas.evidence: h.GetCache() returned a Null (5140) with ~30 child SplineObjects (5101) of varying point counts (2 to 699).
gotchas.workaround: Walk cache.GetDown() and accumulate point counts across children to assess output size.

rebuild_recipe: 2. Build classic-OM input pipeline as children: Text Spline → Connect Splines → Spline Mask → optional Tracer/Fracture/Connect Instance for animated paths.
3. Add 1-3 doc-level Field objects. Bind to input spline's vertex selection via FieldList tag.
a. children@ reads host children (the input spline tree).
b. splitspline@ divides the input into per-segment chunks.
c. lineget@ reads topology (segment counts, ranges).
d. getvertexselectiondata@ reads field-painted weights per vertex.
e. loopcarriedvalue@ iterates per-segment: distance@ → maprange@ → curl-point generation.
f. append@ accumulates Points + Segments arrays across all segments.
g. gradient@ + color@ apply per-vertex color along the output.
h. assembler@ produces multi-segment spline from Points + Segments.
5. Keyframe Field positions in OM for animated growth — OR position fields statically for an artist-controllable look.
6. Expose 8-12 root-direct AM ports (with floatingio wrappers ideally for clarity).

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: field_driven_spline_growth
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Add field-driven decorations (curls, sprouts, branches, ornaments) along an input spline. Grows where Field weight is high, quiet where weight is low. Stateless — animates via OM Field motion (keyframes or interactive).
nodes: children (read input spline children from OM)
splitspline (divide input into per-segment chunks — Divide Spline)
getvertexselectiondata (read field-painted weights per vertex — Points Info)
loopcarriedvalue (per-segment iteration; produces curl points in body)
assembler (Points + Segments → multi-segment spline — Assemble Spline)
exposed_params: Curl Amplitude (Float) — peak curl size at field max
Spline Object (Object) — root-direct binding to input spline tree
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Generalizes far beyond text ornament: ANY 'decorate the path' task. Vine on a winding road, ornamental flourishes on logo splines, sprouting branches on a tree skeleton, ivy along mesh edges, lightning bolt branches. Stateless animation via Field keyframes is artist-friendly: no graph state to debug, no sequential-stepping requirement.
secondary_recipes: R16_om_driven_animation — keyframe Fields in OM, let graph stay stateless
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: children@, splitspline@, lineget@, assembler@ not yet exposed via cinema4d-mcp scene_nodes_add_node
Per-vertex color emission via gradient@ + color@ pattern needs recipe
Methodology gotcha #58: position fields manually to validate field-driven scenes

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: splitspline@ is the per-spline-segment primitive — like explode_islands@ for meshes. Recipe-worthy.
lineget@ reads spline topology — useful for graphs that need to know segment structure.
OM-driven animation is a stateless pattern — no memory@ needed if all per-frame variation comes from keyframed Fields or classic-generator outputs.
Classic-OM generators (Connect/Tracer/Spline Mask/Fracture) compose naturally with Scene Nodes hosts. Recipes should make this composition easy.
Same assembler@ Points+Segments pattern as scene 05 — confirming R12 is universal.
METHODOLOGY: For Field-driven scenes, manually position fields into the input volume to validate behavior. Don't conclude scene-broken without trying this first.

frames: frames/fields_centered_topdown.png (manual: fields centered on spline — full ornament growth)
frames/fields_left.png (manual: fields shifted left — growth on left half)
frames/fields_right.png (manual: fields shifted right — growth on right half)
